items.title: Villa interested in Mateta - Wednesday's gossip
items.summary: Aston Villa explore Jean-Philippe Mateta and Tammy Abraham moves, Manchester United decline Ajax's Manuel Ugarte approach and Bournemouth fight to keep Marcos Senesi.

items.title: Frank on how he is managing the pressure at Spurs
items.summary: Thomas Frank feels his Tottenham Hotspur side are getting better and that he deserves patience following their two goal win over 10-man Borussia Dortmund in the Champions League.

items.title: Ajax zorgt voor coëfficiëntenopsteker, maar concurrentie wint ook
items.summary: Ajax zorgde dinsdagavond voor kleine opsteker in de Nederlandse coëfficiëntenstrijd. De Amsterdammers zorgden voor 0,333 punt en mag door die zege nog hopen op de tussenronde en daarmee 1,00 bonuspunt. De concurrenten van Nederland pakten echter ook punten.

items.title: Inkomsten Ajax in Champions League boven veertig miljoen na zege bij Villarreal
items.summary: Ajax kon na de ontsnapping bij Villarreal in de Champions League een tweede winstpremie bijschrijven. Daardoor steeg het bedrag dat de club tot dusver incasseerde boven de veertig miljoen euro uit. Dát kan nog verder oplopen met een resultaat tegen Olympiakos, het bereiken van de de tussenronde én zo hoog mogelijk eindigen in de competitiefase.

items.title: Ajax' kans op bereiken tussenronde Champions League groeit naar 11,5 procent
items.summary: Ajax mag blijven dromen van de tussenronde van de Champions League dankzij Oliver Edvardsen. De aanvaller schiet in de slotfase de Amsterdammers op een 1-2 voorsprong bij Villarreal, dat op voorsprong kwam en Oscar Gloukh de 1-1 zag maken. Lees alles terug in onze VI Live.

items.title: Regeer groeit in leidersrol bij Ajax en gaat in op businessclub-akkefietje
items.summary: Youri Regeer was na afloop van de zege op Villarreal ( 1-2 ) tevreden over zijn nieuwe rol als leiderstype binnen het elftal en ging in op het akkefietje van afgelopen weekend in de businessclub van Ajax.

items.title: Haaland slams 'embarrassing' Man City UCL loss
items.summary: - Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola said "everything is going wrong" for his team after being on the end of a stunning...

items.title: Dominant Arsenal beat Inter, cruise into last 16
items.summary: Gabriel Jesus struck a first-half brace to fire an unstoppable Arsenal to an impressive 3-1 victory against Inter Milan and confirm their place in the last 16 of the Champions League.

items.title: Mbappé double leads 6-goal Madrid rout of Monaco
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé punished former club Monaco as Real Madrid romped to a 6-1 Champions League victory at the Santiago Bernabeu.

items.title: Grim keek nog niet naar CL-ranglijst: 'Met goed gevoel naar volgende wedstrijd'
items.summary: Fred Grim kijkt met een goed gevoel terug op de wedstrijd tegen Villarreal in de Champions League . Dat Ajax nog altijd kans maakt op plaatsing voor de tussenronde van de Champions League, deed hem verder weinig.

items.title: Northern Sights: Hauge's howitzer sends Bodø/Glimt to shock upset of Man City
items.summary: Jens Petter Hauge's stunning strike from range headlined Bodø/Glimt's astonishing 3-1 victory over powerhouse Manchester City.

items.title: Champions League as it happened: Mbappé, Jesus with braces to their name
items.summary: Enjoy all that happened on Tuesday in the first UEFA Champions League matchday of 2026.

items.title: Mbappé and Vinícius lead Real Madrid resurgence as Monaco are hit for six
items.summary: The scars remain and there is much to be fixed still but this was a significant step towards reconciliation and something of a statement too. Not just in the goals they scored, but the reaction to them. Nine days after Xabi Alonso’s sacking, six after their captain said they had hit rock bottom with Copa del Rey elimination and three after the protest of a generation, white hankies and whistles greeting the players and even the president, there were songs and support at last as Real Madrid defeated Monaco 6-1. Kylian Mbappé, Franco Mastantuono and Jude Bellingham all scored, while there was also an own goal from Monaco’s Thilo Kehrer. The Englishman celebrated his with a drinking celebration following rumours about his supposed off-field habits and accusations about his part in Alonso’s sacking. He had already referred to that by posting: “Honestly, what a load of shit.” Smiling now, this response was even more pointed, and done from a position of power, at the end of an impressive performance from all of them. Continue reading...

items.title: Gabriel Jesus stakes claim at Inter as Arsenal celebrate qualification in style
items.summary: Mission accomplished for Arsenal. A seventh win out of seven ensured Mikel Arteta’s side will head straight into the last 16 of this competition as one of the favourites for the Champions League after Gabriel Jesus scored twice – including their 19th goal of the season from a corner – to see off last year’s beaten finalists. It means that as well as getting one back over an Inter side that beat them here 14 months ago, Arsenal have surpassed their longest winning streak at this level. While Manchester City’s surprise defeat in Norway in the earlier kick-off had removed any jeopardy about them progressing, this was more evidence of the ruthless streak they have developed under Arteta. The only blot on the copybook in a fourth successive away game in four different competitions was Petar Sucic’s equaliser in the first half after Jesus had given them an early lead, although this was all about the Brazil striker even after the substitute Viktor Gyökeres sealed the points late on with a classy finish. Continue reading...

items.title: Solanke leads Tottenham past 10-man Dortmund to offer relief for Frank
items.summary: It was a contender for shock result of the season. Nobody had given Tottenham any hope after Saturday’s Premier League disaster here against West Ham, one which had come coated in vitriol for Thomas Frank. The fans had demanded his immediate removal as the manager only for him to stagger on. The execution was stayed. But here were Borussia Dortmund, the Bundesliga’s second-placed team, who had lost only three games all season, to apply the final cut. Frank could see the bones in his resources – 13 players unavailable, only 11 established outfielders from which to select. Continue reading...

items.title: Jesus and Gyokeres give Arteta selection headache as Arsenal beat Inter
items.summary: Arsenal celebrated their qualification to the Champions League last 16 in style as they beat Inter Milan courtesy of Gabriel Jesus and Viktor Gyokeres.

items.title: 'Battered in Bodo' - is this more than just a blip for Man City?
items.summary: Manchester City suffered their very own "hell of a beating" moment in Norway on Tuesday - now Pep Guardiola and his players must turn around their fortunes in an increasingly trying January.

items.title: Turning point? Spurs relieve pressure on Frank with impressive Dortmund win
items.summary: Patched-up Spurs' best 45 minutes of the season relieved some of the mounting pressure on Thomas Frank with a 2-0 win over 10-man Borussia Dortmund.

items.title: 'Everything is going wrong' - Guardiola on Bodo/Glimt upset
items.summary: Pep Guardiola responds to his team's second defeat in four days as Manchester City lose at Bodo/Glimt in the Champions League.

items.title: Pep: 'Everything is going wrong' for Man City
items.summary: Pep Guardiola says Manchester City have a feeling that "everything is going wrong" as he called on his "fragile" players to "change the dynamic quick" after their shock Champions League defeat at Bodo/Glimt.
